prompt: A vast traditional Korean dining table stretching from foreground into deep background, single continuous table, elevated angle. The far background end: elderly Korean family members crowded together, warm golden candlelight, full rice bowls, overlapping shoulders, quiet conversation, blurred by depth of field. Moving toward the foreground: chairs grow sparse, settings fewer. At the very nearest end of the same table: one small wooden child's chair, pulled slightly out as if waiting. One pristine white empty rice bowl, never filled. A single white candle, lit but burning alone. No person. The bowl is immaculate — untouched, waiting for someone who will not come. Cold blue-grey light at the foreground, warm amber deep in background. Extreme cinematic depth of field. Painterly realism, Edward Hopper and Felix Vallotton. No text, no labels, no axes, no charts, no infographic elements. Single continuous frame. no separate tables, no two tables, no adult chair at foreground, no dirty bowl, no used bowl, no bar chart, no graph, no text overlay, no multiple panels, no flags, no maps
dataset_name: Population by Five-Year Age Group - Our World in Data
insight: In 2023, South Korea had the world's lowest fertility rate at 0.72 — its population pyramid is widest among people in their 40s–50s and nearly empty at the bottom, meaning 100 Koreans alive today will leave only 5 descendants four generations from now.
